title: Dropdown & Popup Portal Pattern Rules
description: Rules for creating Dropdowns and Popups inside Modals using React Portal to avoid Overflow and Positioning issues

problem.title: Cutting Problem in Dropdowns inside Modals
problem.description: When creating a dropdown or popup inside a Modal, the element gets cut due to overflow-y-auto from the Modal
symptoms: The dropdown appears cut or incomplete
Part of the list is hidden or inaccessible

rootCauses.explanation: The Modal uses overflow-y-auto which cuts any element that goes outside its boundaries
Using position: absolute inside a Modal makes the dropdown limited by content boundaries
The Modal creates a new stacking context which limits the z-index effect
Using left instead of right in Arabic leads to alignment issues

realExample.issue: Audience choice box was cut and not fully visible
realExample.screenshot: The user sees only part of the dropdown

solution.title: React Portal Pattern for Dropdowns
solution.strategy: Using React Portal to move the dropdown completely out of the Modal context

steps.action: Using useRef for the button and the dropdown
steps.code:
const buttonRef = useRef<HTMLButtonElement>(null);
const dropdownRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);

steps.action: Using fixed positioning with high z-index
steps.code:
className="fixed z-[9999]"
style={{ top: position.top, right: position.right }}

rules.title: Using Portal for Dropdowns inside Modals
rules.description: Any dropdown or popup inside a Modal or any container with overflow must use React Portal
rules.rationale: To avoid content cutting due to overflow constraints

problems: Will be cut by parent overflow

rules.title: Calculating position dynamically
rules.description: The dropdown position must be calculated based on the actual button location on the screen
rules.rationale: To ensure the dropdown appears in the correct position regardless of the Parent location

required: Calculating top and right/left based on viewport

considerations.viewport: Check that the dropdown does not go off-screen
considerations.scrolling: Recalculate position on scroll if necessary
considerations.resize: Listen to window resize event for updates

rules.title: Using Fixed Positioning
rules.description: The dropdown in Portal must use position: fixed, not absolute
rules.rationale: fixed positioning makes the element completely independent of parent positioning context

badExample.problem: absolute is affected by parent positioning

rules.title: RTL Support in Positioning
rules.description: In Arabic applications (RTL), right should be used instead of left
rules.rationale: To ensure correct alignment in Arabic

implementation.detection: Use dir attribute or CSS direction to determine orientation

rules.title: Click Outside Handler
rules.description: Clicks outside the dropdown must be handled correctly when using Portal
rules.rationale: The element is outside the original DOM tree, needs event listener on document

required: useEffect with event listener on document
Check both the button and the dropdown

pitfalls: Not checking the button causes it to close when clicked
Using click instead of mousedown may cause timing issues

rules.title: Z-Index Management
rules.description: Use high z-index (9999 or higher) for Portal elements
rules.rationale: To ensure the dropdown appears above all other elements
rules.note: Make sure no conflict with z-index of other elements in the app

rules.title: SSR Compatibility
rules.description: Check for document presence before using Portal
rules.rationale: In SSR, document is not available during server rendering
rules.alternative: Use useEffect to delay rendering until client-side

rules.title: Animation Support
rules.description: When adding animations, make sure to use AnimatePresence with Portal
rules.rationale: Framer Motion needs AnimatePresence for exit animations

rules.title: Viewport Boundaries Check
rules.description: Check that the dropdown does not go off-screen
rules.rationale: To improve user experience on small screens

rules.title: Accessibility Considerations
rules.description: Add appropriate ARIA attributes for the dropdown
rules.rationale: To support screen readers and keyboard navigation

items.task: Add state for position and isOpen

codeTemplate.title: Dropdown Component Template
codeTemplate.usage: Use this template as a starting point for any dropdown or popup inside a Modal

commonMistakes.consequence: Memory leaks and event listeners accumulate
commonMistakes.fix: Always add return function to removeEventListener

commonMistakes.consequence: The dropdown does not appear in the correct position
The dropdown appears on the wrong side in Arabic
commonMistakes.fix: Use right instead of left with correct calculation

commonMistakes.consequence: The dropdown disappears behind other elements

commonMistakes.mistake: Not checking for document in SSR

commonMistakes.consequence: Wrong position if the button moves

manual: Open the dropdown inside Modal - it must appear completely
Click outside the dropdown - it must close
Try on different screens (mobile, tablet, desktop)
scroll the page - ensure correct position
Open multiple dropdowns together - check z-index

notes: Portal rendering is faster than re-rendering inside Modal
Use useMemo for dropdown content if complex
Avoid re-calculating position on every render
Use CSS transforms instead of top/left for animations

automated.eslint: Create custom rule to detect dropdowns without Portal inside components with overflow

codeReview.checklist: Review every new dropdown/popup according to the Checklist above

migration.title: Migrating Existing Dropdowns
steps: Check if they are inside Modals or overflow containers
